metadata.title: Delete 80% of Your Network and It Runs No Faster

beats.narration_text: Vanakkam, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. You pruned four-fifths of the weights, accuracy barely moved — and inference time didn't change at all.

props.command: I pruned 80% of my model's weights and it's no faster. What's happening?
output: Scattered zeros still live in a full-size matrix. The kernel multiplies every element. Only removing whole channels actually shrinks the compute.

beats.narration_text: The book's pruning comparison: unstructured on the left, scattered zeros the hardware ignores. Structured on the right — whole channels gone, the matrix actually shrinks.

Unstructured pruning: you set eighty percent of individual weights to zero. The matrix is still 8 by 8. The GEMM kernel doesn't know which values are zero — it multiplies all 64 cells anyway. Your flash shrinks because zeros compress. Your SRAM shrinks slightly. Your latency: unchanged. The work the kernel does is identical.

Structured pruning: remove whole channels. The matrix doesn't have zeros — it's physically smaller. Was 8 by 8. Now 5 by 5. That's what the kernel sees. Fewer multiplications. Smaller SRAM footprint. Real latency reduction. The terracotta rows are gone — not zeroed, removed. The matrix shrank.

Side by side at 80% pruning. Unstructured: flash down eighty percent, latency unchanged, SRAM unchanged. Structured: flash down eighty percent, latency down eighty percent, SRAM down eighty percent. Same pruning ratio — completely different runtime impact. If you want speed, you have to remove whole channels.

Your turn. Ask Claude: I'm about to prune my model to open bracket target pct close bracket percent of its parameters. Walk me through: one — the difference between unstructured and structured pruning in terms of flash, SRAM, and latency impact on a Cortex-M target. Two — how to check if my TFLite Micro build actually benefits from structured pruning. Three — the recommended order: prune, then fine-tune, then quantize — and why. Four — what accuracy drop to expect at my target rate.

props.command: I'm about to prune my model to [target_pct]% of its parameters. Walk me through: (1) the difference between unstructured and structured pruning in terms of flash/SRAM/latency impact on a Cortex-M target, (2) how to check if my TFLite Micro build actually benefits from structured pruning, (3) the recommended order: prune → fine-tune → quantize, and why, (4) what accuracy drop to expect at my target rate.
output: Unstructured 80%: flash↓, latency same. Structured 80%: flash↓, latency↓, SRAM↓. Only removing whole channels shrinks the GEMM kernel.

props.title: Delete 80% of Your Network and It Runs No Faster
props.subline: embedded ai · liam, in for bear
